title: corynocarpus cribbianus
intro: Corynocarpus cribbianus, also known as the New Zealand laurel or karaka, is a tree native to New Zealand, belonging to the Corynocarpaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The seeds and leaves of the corynocarpus cribbianus plant have been found to have health benefits such as lowering cholesterol levels, reducing blood pressure, and protecting against cancer. Traditional herbal preparations include making a tea from the leaves or seeds, which can be used to treat various ailments, or using the seeds in a poultice to relieve skin irritations.
